metadata.description: Curated disease therapy adequacy and severity classifications
metadata.methodology: Manual curation based on BUILD_SPEC.md therapeutic adequacy definitions

hepatitis-c.evidence: Direct-acting antivirals (DAA) achieve cure in >95% of cases

multiple-sclerosis.evidence: DMARDs and monoclonal antibodies reduce progression, some achieve NEDA (no evidence of disease activity)

rheumatoid-arthritis.evidence: Biologics (TNF inhibitors, JAK inhibitors) achieve remission in 30-50% of patients

crohns-disease.evidence: Biologics (TNF inhibitors, anti-IL12/23) achieve sustained remission

ulcerative-colitis.evidence: Biologics enable sustained remission; surgery (colectomy) is curative but morbid

hivaids.evidence: Antiretroviral therapy (ART) achieves undetectable viral load in >95% of patients on treatment

diabetes-type-1.evidence: Insulin therapy is lifelong; no disease-modifying agents prevent beta cell loss

hypertension.evidence: Antihypertensive drugs manage blood pressure but do not reverse underlying arterial disease

asthma.evidence: Inhalers (beta-2 agonists, corticosteroids) control symptoms but do not cure airway inflammation

migraine.evidence: Triptans, NSAIDs, CGRP inhibitors treat acute episodes; no cure

anxiety.evidence: SSRIs and benzodiazepines manage symptoms; psychological therapy is primary treatment

depression.evidence: SSRIs and psychotherapy manage symptoms; high relapse rates off treatment

adhd.evidence: Stimulants and atomoxetine improve focus during treatment; symptoms return when stopped

acne.evidence: Topical/systemic antibiotics and retinoids control symptoms; isotretinoin is most effective but teratogenic

notes: This is a preliminary curation layer. Full RTT-DB will include: phase_1 safety characterization, sponsor_supply_posture, and Montana-only eligibility assessment per BUILD_SPEC.md stages 3-6.
